arts-literature-starter.opentdb.b395331d.explanation: David Tennant played the title role in a Royal Shakespeare Company production of Hamlet.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.b395331d.details: His performance opened in 2008 and later became a television film, pairing Tennant with Patrick Stewart as Claudius.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f352307d.explanation: Dr. Seuss wrote and illustrated Green Eggs and Ham.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f352307d.details: The 1960 beginner book famously uses a vocabulary of only 50 different words.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0a0325e2.explanation: The traditional Shakespeare canon contains 37 plays, although attribution debates can change the count.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0a0325e2.details: Modern editions sometimes include collaborative works such as The Two Noble Kinsmen, so totals may vary by editorial standard.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0332d880.explanation: In Shakespeare's Julius Caesar, Caesar's famous final words are “Et tu, Brute?”
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0332d880.details: The Latin means “You too, Brutus?” and dramatizes Caesar's shock at seeing Brutus among his assassins.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.efd1d835.explanation: Stephen King published several early novels under the name Richard Bachman.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.efd1d835.details: The pseudonym let King release more books and test whether his success depended on reputation rather than the writing itself.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.8b4a6252.explanation: Mycroft Holmes is Sherlock Holmes's older brother.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.8b4a6252.details: Mycroft has formidable deductive powers but prefers government work and intellectual comfort to Sherlock's active investigations.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.acbe94dd.explanation: Origami is the Japanese art of folding paper into decorative forms.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.acbe94dd.details: Its name combines ori, meaning folding, with kami, meaning paper; traditional designs are made without cutting or glue.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.230cecf7.explanation: Fluffy is Hagrid's three-headed dog in Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.230cecf7.details: Like the mythological Cerberus, Fluffy guards an entrance; music causes him to fall asleep.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.524fc4c7.explanation: Sir Handel was originally named Falcon in The Railway Series.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.524fc4c7.details: The narrow-gauge engine was renamed after arriving on the Skarloey Railway, where his new name honored the railway's owner.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.54620ad4.explanation: SCP-173 was the first entry in the collaborative SCP Foundation fiction project.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.54620ad4.details: The concrete statue that moves when unobserved established the clinical report style later used throughout the shared universe.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.8229f0a0.explanation: Captain Nemo commands the submarine Nautilus in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.8229f0a0.details: Jules Verne's title describes distance traveled beneath the seas, not a depth of 20,000 leagues.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f57c1d83.explanation: Harry Potter's father is James Potter.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f57c1d83.details: James and Lily Potter were killed by Voldemort when Harry was an infant; James's friends called him Prongs.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f0e9bf69.explanation: James Bond creator Ian Fleming wrote Chitty-Chitty-Bang-Bang.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f0e9bf69.details: The children's novel grew from stories Fleming told his son and features a magical racing car rather than a spy.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.66a1d49d.explanation: The Lord of the Rings is among the best-selling novels ever published.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.66a1d49d.details: Exact rankings depend on whether religious texts, series, and verified sales estimates are included, so “world's best-selling book” is an ambiguous label.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.a133103f.explanation: The Ninth Wave was painted by Russian-Armenian artist Ivan Aivazovsky, not Vincent van Gogh.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.a133103f.details: Aivazovsky specialized in seascapes; the painting shows shipwreck survivors facing an immense wave at sunrise.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.2c144d7c.explanation: West Side Story reimagines Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet in twentieth-century New York.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.2c144d7c.details: The rival Montagues and Capulets become the Jets and Sharks, while Tony and Maria parallel Romeo and Juliet.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0dc6d01f.explanation: The Starry Night depicts the view and imagined night sky associated with Van Gogh's asylum at Saint-Rémy.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0dc6d01f.details: Van Gogh painted it in 1889, combining observed landscape elements with an expressive, swirling sky.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0ae97690.explanation: Salvador Dalí painted Swans Reflecting Elephants, Sleep, and The Persistence of Memory.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0ae97690.details: These works use dreamlike transformations and impossible imagery characteristic of Dalí's Surrealism.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ad72a00b.explanation: Vincent van Gogh painted The Starry Night in 1889.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ad72a00b.details: The work's energetic brushstrokes and intense color make it a defining example of Post-Impressionist expression.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.827857d5.explanation: Michelangelo painted The Creation of Adam on the Sistine Chapel ceiling.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.827857d5.details: The near-touching hands of God and Adam became one of the most recognizable images of Renaissance art.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f6b4db49.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f6b4db49.details: The portrait is celebrated for its enigmatic expression, atmospheric background, and Leonardo's subtle sfumato transitions.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.016ef242.explanation: Michelangelo painted the Sistine Chapel's famous ceiling frescoes.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.016ef242.details: He worked on the ceiling from 1508 to 1512 and later painted The Last Judgment on the altar wall.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0e608c2e.details: Completed in the early 1500s, the statue shows the biblical hero before his battle with Goliath.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.9a9e41bc.explanation: J. K. Rowling wrote the Harry Potter series.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.9a9e41bc.details: The seven main novels follow Harry from his discovery of the wizarding world through his conflict with Voldemort.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.52b03238.explanation: Lin-Manuel Miranda wrote the music and lyrics for In the Heights.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.52b03238.details: The musical centers on a Dominican American neighborhood in Washington Heights and preceded Miranda's Hamilton.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.749102c4.explanation: Tony Kushner wrote Angels in America.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.749102c4.details: The two-part drama examines AIDS, politics, religion, and identity in 1980s America and won the Pulitzer Prize for Drama.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f6c8a9e3.explanation: The supercomputer Deep Thought calculates the answer to life, the universe, and everything as 42.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f6c8a9e3.details: Douglas Adams makes the joke deeper by revealing that nobody actually knows the corresponding ultimate question.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.df26da14.explanation: Eric Arthur Blair wrote under the pen name George Orwell.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.df26da14.details: Orwell is best known for Animal Farm and Nineteen Eighty-Four, works critical of authoritarianism and political manipulation.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.d688fc10.explanation: The Chronicles of Narnia consists of seven books.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.d688fc10.details: C. S. Lewis published them between 1950 and 1956; modern editions often arrange them by internal chronology rather than publication order.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.abd02539.explanation: In Jeff Wayne's musical War of the Worlds, the chances of Martians coming to Earth are described as “a million to one.”
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.abd02539.details: The repeated line makes the invasion more dramatic by contrasting human certainty with the catastrophe that follows.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.a5b4e3b5.explanation: In Michael Crichton's Jurassic Park novel, John Hammond is killed by a group of Procompsognathus.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.a5b4e3b5.details: The small scavenging dinosaurs overwhelm the injured Hammond; the film changes his fate and allows him to survive.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.a044d213.explanation: Winny Hathersham is not one of Wyrd Sisters' three central witches.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.a044d213.details: Terry Pratchett's trio is Granny Weatherwax, Nanny Ogg, and Magrat Garlick, loosely echoing Shakespeare's three witches.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.7e20b018.explanation: A Ravenclaw student must answer a riddle to enter the house common room.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.7e20b018.details: Unlike Gryffindor and Slytherin, Ravenclaw uses intellectual challenges rather than a fixed password, reflecting the house's value of wit.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0cf1d877.explanation: Marcellus says “Something is rotten in the state of Denmark” in Hamlet.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0cf1d877.details: He speaks after Hamlet follows the Ghost, capturing the play's atmosphere of political and moral corruption.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.653a1da4.details: Queen Gertrude describes Ophelia drowning after falling from a willow tree, leaving open questions about accident and suicide.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.4a393d01.explanation: Andrew Lloyd Webber's Cats is based on T. S. Eliot's Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.4a393d01.details: The musical adapts Eliot's playful poems about distinctive cats, adding music and a loose theatrical storyline.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.3363f2df.details: The dystopian novel imagines firemen burning books in a society dominated by censorship and shallow mass entertainment.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.b872ddce.explanation: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry wrote The Little Prince.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.b872ddce.details: The philosophical novella uses a child's interplanetary journey to explore love, responsibility, loneliness, and adult priorities.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ef68cf0f.explanation: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels is set on the French Riviera.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ef68cf0f.details: Its con artists compete to swindle wealthy visitors in the glamorous resort setting around Beaumont-sur-Mer.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.e8c58e62.explanation: John Steinbeck wrote Of Mice and Men.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.e8c58e62.details: The novella follows migrant ranch workers George and Lennie during the Great Depression and examines friendship, dreams, and vulnerability.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ebc2a564.explanation: Act I of the musical Chess opens at a world championship in Merano, Italy.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ebc2a564.details: The setting reflects the real 1981 World Chess Championship, held in the northern Italian resort town.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.78c25b77.explanation: Jim Butcher's Dresden Files follows wizard and private investigator Harry Dresden in modern Chicago.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.78c25b77.details: The series combines hard-boiled detective fiction with urban fantasy, supernatural politics, and magical crime solving.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.4fba1a09.explanation: Numbers is the fourth book of the Old Testament.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.4fba1a09.details: It follows Genesis, Exodus, and Leviticus and takes its English name from censuses of the Israelites.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.9b43450b.explanation: The Jurassic Park novel describes Toyota Land Cruisers as the park's touring vehicles.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.9b43450b.details: The 1993 film instead made its automated Ford Explorers visually iconic, while gas-powered Jeeps served staff transport.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ac5bbab7.explanation: Salvador Dalí was Spanish, born in Catalonia.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ac5bbab7.details: He became one of Surrealism's best-known figures through dream imagery, technical precision, and a deliberately theatrical public persona.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.064ed814.explanation: “Hell is other people” comes from Jean-Paul Sartre's play No Exit.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.064ed814.details: The line concerns the torment of being fixed in other people's judgments, not simply a dislike of company.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.17e30bdf.explanation: Harry Potter plays Seeker for the Gryffindor Quidditch team.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.17e30bdf.details: The Seeker tries to catch the Golden Snitch, an action worth 150 points in the original rules and usually ending the match.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.8cb684e9.explanation: Carmen was Georges Bizet's final opera.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.8cb684e9.details: Bizet died only months after its 1875 premiere, before the work achieved the international popularity it later gained.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.90f17224.explanation: The dolphins leave Earth saying, “So long, and thanks for all the fish.”
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.90f17224.details: In The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, dolphins understand the planet's danger but humans mistake their warnings for tricks.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.286348d7.explanation: Mary Ann Evans published fiction under the name George Eliot.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.286348d7.details: Her novels, including Middlemarch and Silas Marner, are known for psychological depth and close observation of provincial society.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.bf681f54.explanation: Macbeth is generally dated to about 1606.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.bf681f54.details: The play's interest in Scottish kingship and witchcraft fits the reign of James I, who became England's king in 1603.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f7e9e5f6.explanation: Andy Warhol called his New York studio The Factory.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f7e9e5f6.details: The studio became a center for Warhol's screen prints, films, music collaborations, and celebrity-filled social scene.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0b707393.explanation: Georges Seurat developed images from small dots of contrasting color, a method called Pointillism.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0b707393.details: Viewed from a distance, the separate marks blend optically; A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte is the classic example.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.db80087a.explanation: John Grisham's stolen-manuscript idea became the novel Camino Island.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.db80087a.details: The 2017 book departs from his usual courtroom focus and follows a rare-book dealer, a writer, and stolen Fitzgerald manuscripts.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.7c3fbc24.explanation: Arthur Conan Doyle was born in Edinburgh, Scotland, not England.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.7c3fbc24.details: Although Sherlock Holmes is closely associated with London, his creator was Scottish and studied medicine at the University of Edinburgh.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.c0dbc8c0.explanation: The Back Alley is not a song from West Side Story.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.c0dbc8c0.details: The musical's famous numbers include Maria, Tonight, America, Somewhere, and Gee, Officer Krupke.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.d2f66e01.explanation: YouTuber Joe Sugg wrote the Username: Evie graphic novel series.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.d2f66e01.details: The story follows a teenager who enters a digital world created by her late father.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.3096afed.explanation: Copic is a well-known brand of refillable alcohol-based art markers.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.3096afed.details: Illustrators value the markers for blendable ink and replaceable nibs; the brand originated in Japan.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.49307361.explanation: James Patterson created the Maximum Ride series.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.49307361.details: The young-adult novels follow genetically altered children with wings who escape from the scientists who created them.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.cf8b2fdf.explanation: Rent won the Tony Award for Best Musical in 1996.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.cf8b2fdf.details: Jonathan Larson's musical adapts themes from Puccini's La bohème to artists living in New York during the AIDS crisis.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.5d49dca4.explanation: The Black Sea is not a painting by German Romantic artist Caspar David Friedrich.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.5d49dca4.details: Friedrich is known for contemplative landscapes such as Wanderer above the Sea of Fog and The Monk by the Sea.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.7501b6c1.explanation: Cut time is the 2/2 time signature.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.7501b6c1.details: It has two half-note beats per measure and is marked with a vertical line through the common-time C symbol.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.5478c2d7.explanation: Salvador Dalí designed the Chupa Chups logo.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.5478c2d7.details: Dalí created the flower-shaped design in 1969 and suggested placing it on top of the wrapper so it remained visible.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f13e9741.explanation: Alex Lacamoire served as Hamilton's music director, orchestrator, and conductor.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f13e9741.details: His orchestration helped translate Lin-Manuel Miranda's mix of hip-hop, R&B, pop, and traditional musical theater to Broadway.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.894b412b.details: The vast black-and-white mural responds to the 1937 bombing of the Basque town and became an antiwar symbol.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.5653ded4.explanation: Daveed Diggs originated both Lafayette and Thomas Jefferson in Hamilton.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.5653ded4.details: The dual casting lets one actor play a French ally in Act I and an American political rival in Act II.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.6abb8047.explanation: Hans Christian Andersen wrote The Little Match Girl.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.6abb8047.details: The bleak 1845 tale contrasts a poor child's suffering with comforting visions she sees while lighting her matches.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.df976a20.explanation: More Shakespeare plays are set in present-day Italy than in any country except England.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.df976a20.details: Italian settings include Verona, Venice, Rome, Padua, and Sicily, offering distant stages for romance, politics, and family conflict.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.e06e5a8a.explanation: Albrecht Dürer was born and died in Nuremberg, Germany.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.e06e5a8a.details: The Renaissance artist made the city a center of printmaking through technically remarkable woodcuts and engravings.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f3b1511f.explanation: Edvard Munch created four painted or pastel versions of The Scream.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f3b1511f.details: He also made a lithographic version, which explains why counts differ when prints are included.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.b08b8c32.explanation: The Macbeth witches add “eye of newt” to their cauldron.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.b08b8c32.details: The phrase appears in the famous “double, double toil and trouble” spell and has become shorthand for a witch's potion.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.d79807a7.explanation: Dumbledore's full name is Albus Percival Wulfric Brian Dumbledore.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.d79807a7.details: The unusually long name appears in the books and helps distinguish his formal identity from the simple name used in conversation.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.fd80e0af.explanation: Tommy Brock is a badger in Beatrix Potter's stories.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.fd80e0af.details: He is the rough antagonist of The Tale of Mr. Tod, in which he steals Benjamin Bunny's children.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0086f086.explanation: Cornelius Fudge's middle name is Oswald.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.0086f086.details: Fudge serves as Minister for Magic and becomes an obstacle when he refuses to accept Voldemort's return.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.68a41b72.explanation: In The Lies of Locke Lamora, Lamora means “shadow” in the Throne Therin language.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.68a41b72.details: The meaning suits Locke's hidden identity and his life as a thief operating through deception.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.e0c8993e.explanation: Magic: The Gathering's Antiquities, Ice Age, and Alliances stories take place on Terisiare.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.e0c8993e.details: Terisiare is a continent on Dominaria, the plane central to much of Magic's early history.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ed905b52.explanation: Nathanael West's The Day of the Locust includes a character named Homer Simpson.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.ed905b52.details: The novel appeared in 1939, decades before The Simpsons, and portrays alienation and desperation around Hollywood.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.12fb6e4a.explanation: Piet Mondrian was a leader of Neoplasticism, closely associated with the De Stijl movement.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.12fb6e4a.details: The style reduces painting to straight lines, primary colors, and neutral black, white, and gray.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.66194afb.explanation: The Starry Night belongs to Post-Impressionism.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.66194afb.details: Van Gogh moved beyond naturalistic color and perspective to express emotion through intensified color and rhythmic brushwork.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.2f5a5e39.explanation: French sculptor Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi designed the Statue of Liberty.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.2f5a5e39.details: Gustave Eiffel's engineering team designed the internal framework, while Bartholdi shaped the colossal copper exterior.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.78d701ec.explanation: Hermione Granger's middle name is Jean.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.78d701ec.details: Earlier material used Jane, but J. K. Rowling later established Jean as the canonical middle name.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.e47c1d6b.explanation: Ron Weasley's middle name is Bilius.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.e47c1d6b.details: He was named for his Uncle Bilius, a Weasley relative remembered for eccentric and unfortunate magical incidents.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.7962f510.explanation: The Elves is often identified as Broadway's first long-run musical production.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.7962f510.details: It opened in 1857 and reached 50 performances, a notable run before later productions redefined Broadway longevity.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.56d2e20f.explanation: James and Lily Potter are buried in Godric's Hollow.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.56d2e20f.details: The village is also where they were killed and where Harry survived Voldemort's attack as an infant.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f8f8145e.explanation: Albrecht Dürer created Young Hare in 1502.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.f8f8145e.details: The highly detailed watercolor and body-color study is admired for making an ordinary animal feel vividly present.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.55413466.explanation: Leonardo began the Mona Lisa around 1503–1504, though many scholars believe he continued refining it for years.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.55413466.details: A single “finished” date is therefore uncertain; Leonardo may have worked on the portrait until late in his life.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.212b7406.explanation: The Silence of the Lambs gets its title from Clarice Starling's childhood memory of screaming lambs.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.212b7406.details: She tried and failed to save one lamb, and the memory symbolizes the innocent victims she hopes to rescue.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.9bc831fb.explanation: Friedrich Engels co-wrote The Communist Manifesto with Karl Marx.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.9bc831fb.details: Published in 1848, the pamphlet presents their theory of class struggle and a program for communist political action.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.1cd7dc2a.explanation: Trial of Temperance is not a Shakespeare work.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.1cd7dc2a.details: Shakespeare's accepted canon includes tragedies, comedies, histories, poems, and collaborative plays, but no title by that name.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.a2ce7644.explanation: Charlie does not read The Grapes of Wrath in The Perks of Being a Wallflower.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.a2ce7644.details: His teacher Bill assigns a sequence of classics that helps chart Charlie's intellectual and emotional growth.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.5d0ed185.explanation: Kobicha is a brown shade, not a traditional variation of purple.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.5d0ed185.details: The Japanese color name refers to a kelp-tea brown, while violet, mauve, and similar choices belong to the purple family.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.1aad9f2e.explanation: The stage direction involving two heads and a hand appears in Titus Andronicus.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.1aad9f2e.details: The gruesome entrance fits Shakespeare's bloodiest tragedy, in which severed body parts repeatedly drive revenge and recognition.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.d01e7ab9.explanation: Brendon Urie played Charlie Price in Broadway's Kinky Boots during the summer of 2017.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.d01e7ab9.details: The Panic! at the Disco singer joined the production for a limited run, making his Broadway debut.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.27937af9.explanation: A character identified as the Chorus speaks the prologue to Romeo and Juliet.
arts-literature-starter.opentdb.27937af9.details: The opening sonnet reveals the lovers' fate in advance, shifting the drama from surprise toward tragic inevitability.

arts-literature-starter.opentdb.31fcbe3f.details: The 1851 novel follows Ishmael aboard the Pequod as Captain Ahab pursues the white whale that maimed him.
